heading: Income Tax Act, 2015 (Act 896) — section 51, section 133 and Fifth Schedule slices

metadata.original_publisher: Ghana Publishing Company Limited (Assembly Press), Accra
metadata.slice_note: Discontiguous page_windows capture from the 182-page image-only GRA print of Act 896 (OCR). Page 41 carries section 51 (personal reliefs enabling rule, window stops before Division II Partnerships); pages 103-111 carry section 133 (interpretation, including the currency point definition of one Cedi); page 133 carries the complete Fifth Schedule (personal reliefs, paragraphs 1-3, original 2015 currency-point amounts). Amounts in Fifth Schedule paragraph 1(a) and (c)-(f) were substituted by the Income Tax (Amendment) Act, 2019 (Act 1007) with effect for 2020 onward; cite those amounts from the Act 1007 provision, not this base text. Paragraph 1(b) disability relief (25 percent of assessable income from business or employment) is unamended base text.

or (4) a part of an item described in paragraph (a}; 103 Act 896 INCOME TAX ACT 2015 “asset” includes property of any kind whether tangible or intangible, currency, goodwill, know-how, a right to mcome or future income, a benefit that lasts longer than twelve months, a part of or any right or mterest in, to or over an asset; “associate” means a person who 1s im a controlled relationship with another person and has the meaning assigned in section 128; “business” fa} includes (i) a trade, profession, vocation or isolated arrangement with a business character: and (ii) a past, present or prospective business; but (6) excludes an employment: “capital asset” fa) includes an asset to the extent to which it isemployed in a business or investment; but {o) excludes trading stock or a depreciable asset; “clase company” means a company owned by not more than five persons; "Commussioner-General” means the Commussioner-General appointed under section 13 of the Ghana Revenue Authority Act, 2009 (Act 791); “company” means a company incorporated under the laws of Ghana or elsewhere and includes fa) a friendly society, building society or similar society; fo) a pension fund, provident fund, retirement fund, superannuation fund or similar fund; and fe) a government, a political subdivision of a govern- ment, ora public international organisation but does not include a partnership or a trust, “consideration received" for an asset has the meaning assigned in section 37: “cost in relation to an asset has the meaning assiened in section 36; “currency point” means one Cedi: “debt claim” has the meaning assigned in section 131; 104 INCOME TAX ACT! 2015 Act $96 “debt obligation” has the meaning assigned in section 131, “depreciable asset” (a) means an asset to the extent to which it 1s employed in the production of income from a business and which is likely to lose value because of wear and tear, obsolescence or the effluxion of time; andl (4) does not include goodwill, an interest in land, a membership interest in an entity and trading stock: “derived”, with respect to income or an amount, includes accrued: “dividend” means fa) a payment derived by a member from a company, whether received as a division of profits, in the course of a liquidation or reconstruction, in a reduction of capital or redeemable preference shares or otherwise; fi) an amount treated as dividends in a situation where the Commissioner-General makes a deemed distri- bution of dividends in the case of a close company; and includes a capitalisation of profits fc} whether by way of a bonus share issue, increase in the amount paid-up on shares or otherwise; and f@) whether an amount is distributed or not; but does not include a payment to the extent to which it is fe) matched by a payment made by the member to the company; {f) debited to a capital, share premium or similar account, or fe) otherwise constitutes a final withholding payment or is included in computing the income of the merber; “domestic expenditure” has the meaning assigned in section 130; “employee” means an individual engaged in employment; “employer” means the person who engages or remunerates an employee in employment; “employment” means (a) a position of an individual in the employ of another person; [05 Act 896 INCOME TAX ACT) atts, (i) a position of an individual as manager of an entity other than as partner of a partnership; (c) a position of an individual entitling the individual to a fixed or ascertainable remuneration in respect of serviees performed, and (@) a public office held by an individual; “entity” means a company, partnership or trust, but does not include an individual: “excluded expenditure” has the meaning assigned in section 134); “exempt amount” means an amount exempt by reason of section 7, 37, 59 or 111] or the Sixth Schedule; “expense' means a payment made that reduces the assets of the person making the payment; “final withholding payment" has the meaning assigned in section 11% “financial institution” means (a) a bank regulated under the Banking Act, 2004 {Act 673); fb) a non-banking financial institution regulated under the Non-Banking Financial Institutions Act, 2008 (Act 774; or fe) any other category of person prescribed by Regula- tions; “financial cost” has the meaning assigned in section [31], “financial gain” has the meaning assigned in section 131; “financial instrument” has the meaning assigned in section 131, “gain” in relation to the realisation of an asset or liability has the meaning assigned in section 35; “generally accepted accounting principles” means the generally accepted accounting principles that the Institute of Chartered Accountants (Ghana) may adopt; “interest” includes {a} a payment, in the nature of a discount or premium, made under a debt obligation that is nota return of capital; ro] & Sh INCOME TAX ACT, 2015 Act 896 (6) aswap or other payment functionally equivalent to interest: (¢) a commitment, guarantee or service fee paid in respect-af a debt obligation or swap agreement; and (@) a distbunion by a building society; “investment” includes fa} the owning of one or more assets of a similar nature or that are used in an integrated fashion; or fb) a present, past or prospective investment; but does not include business or employment; “investment asset” (a) includes a capital asset held as part of an invest- ment being shares or securities in a company, a beneficial interest in a trust or an interest in land or buildings, but (®) excludes the primary private residence of an individual, provided the residence has been owned by the individual continuously for the three years before disposal and lived in on a daily basis for at least two of those three years; “lease” means an arrangement providing a person with a tem- porary right in respect of an asset of another person, other than money, and is in the nature of a licence, profit-a-prendre, option, rental agreement, royally agreement or tenancy; “manager”, in relation to an entily fa) means a councillor, director, manager, member, officer or other person who participates or may participate, whether alone or jointly with other per- sons, In making senior management decisions on behalf of the entity; and (bh) includes (i) a partner of a partnership and a trustee of a trust: (ii) a person treated as a manager of an entity by another tax law; (iii) a person in accordance with whose directions and instructions the entity ora person described in the rest of this definition is required or accustomed to act; and 107 Act 896 INCOME TAX ACT, 2015 (iv) a non-resident person with respect to a Ghanaian permanent establishment owned by the person under section 106; “market value” of a payment, asset or liability is determined in accordance with section 26; “member™ in relation to an entity means a person who owns a membership interest im the entity; “membership interest” in an entity means a right, whether of a legal or equitable nature, and is in the nature of a contingent tight to participate in income or capital of the entity, the interest of a partner in a partnership, the interest of a beneficiary in a trust and shares in a company; “Minister” means the Minister responsible for Finance; “natural resource” means minerals, petroleum, water or any other non-living or living resource that may be taken from land or the sea; “natural resource payment” means a payment in the nature of a premium or like amount, for the right to take natural resources from land or the sea or calculated in whole or part by reference to the quantity or value of natural resources taken from the land or the sea: “net cost” for an asset or liability at a particular time is fa} im the case of a depreciable asset, the share of the written down value of the pool to which it belongs at that time apportioned according to the market value of all the assets in the pool; and fb} in the case of any other asset or a liability, the amount by which cumulative costs for the asset or liability exceed cumulative consideration received for the asset or liability to the time; “partnership” means an association of two or more individuals or corporations carrying on business jointly for the purpose of making profit, irrespective of whether the association is recorded in writing; “payment” Includes an amount paid or payable in cash or kind, and the conferring of value or a benefit in any form by one person on another person and is in the nature of fa) the transfer by one person of an asset or moncy to another person or the transfer by another person of a lability to the one person; 108 INCOME TAX ACT, 2015 Act 896 fb) the creation by one person of an asset that on creation is owned by another person or the decrease by one person of a lability owed by another person: (c) the supply of services by one person to another; and fd) the making available of an asset or moncy owned by one person for use by another person or the granting af use of an asset or money to another person; “permanent establishment” means a Ghanaian permanent establishment or foreign permanent establishment within the meanings assigned in section 107; “person” means an individual or entity; “redundancy pay” has the meaning assigned in section 65 of the Labour Act, 2003 (Act 651): “relative” has the meaning assigned in section 128; “relevant financial cost” has the meaning assigned in section 131; “relevant financial gain" has the meaning assigned in section 131; “rent” includes (2) a payment, includmg a payment of a premium or like amount, for the use of or right to use property including equipment of any kind; and ff) a payment for the rendering of, or the undertaking to render, assistance ancillary to a use or a right re- ferred to in paragraph (a): but excludes a natural resource payment or a royalty; “residence” or “resident” with respect to a person is determined in accordance with section 101: “retirement contribution” has the meaning assigned in section 96; “retirement fund” has the meaning assigned in section 95; “retirement payment" has the meaning assigned in section 96; “royalty” includes a payment of a premuum or like amount, derived as consideration for fa) the use of or right to use a copyright of hterary, artistic or scientific work, mcluding cmematograph films, software or video or audio recordings, whether the work is m electronic format or other- Wise; id9 Act 896 INCOME TAX ACT) 205 fb) the use of or right to use a patent, trade mark, design or model, plan, or secret formula or process; fe) the use of or right to use any industrial, commercial, or scientific equipment: f@) the use of or right to use information concerning industial, commercial, or scientific experience; fe) the rendering of or the undertaking to render assis- tance ancillary to a matter referred to in paragraph (a), (), (¢) or (dh, or (a total or partial forbearance with respect to a mat- ter referred to in paragraph fa), (6) fc), fd) or (ek “service fee” means a payment to the extent to which, based on market values, it ig reasonably attributable to services rendered by a business of a person, but excludes interest, rent or a royalty; “shareholder” means a person who is a member of a company; “small scale mining” has the meaning assigned in the Minerals and Mining Act, 2006 (Act 703); “state owned or state sponsored educational institution” means an educational institution fa) established by the Government of Ghana or any politcal division ofthe Government, whether district, municipal or metropolitan authority, and usually tegulated in matters of detail by the government or that division and maintained at public expense by taxation and open, usually without charpe or with minimal charge, to the children of all the residents of that division or other divisions; or (6) belonging to the public and established, conducted or managed under public authonty by the central fovernmment or a politcal division of the central BOVE Men; “trading stock” means assets owned by a person that are sold or intended to be sold in the ordinary course of a business of the person, work in progress on the assets, inventories of matenals to be incorporated into the assets and consumable stores; “trust” means an arrangement under which a trustee holds assets; 110 INCOME TAX ACT, 2013 Act 896 “trustee” means an individual or body corporate holding assets in a fiduciary capacity for the benefit of identifiable persons or for some object permitted by law and whether or nor fa} the assets are held alone or jointly with other indi- () {c) viduals or bodies corporate; or the individual or body corporate is appointed or constituted trustee by personal acts, by will, by order or declaration of a court or by other operation of the law; and includes (i) an executor, administrator, tutor or curator: (it) a liquidator, receiver, trustee in bankruptcy or judicial manager: (i) a person having the administration or control of assets subject to a usufruct, fideicommissum or other Limited interest; (iv) a person who manages the assets of an imea- pacitated individual; and (v) a person who manages assets under a private foundation or other simular arrangement; “underlying ownership” (@) inrelahon to an entity, means membership interests (b) owned in the entity, directly or indirectly through one or more interposed entities, by individuals or by entities in which no person has a membership interest, or in relation to an asset owned by an entity, is deter- mined as though the asset is owned by the persons having underlying ownership of the entity in proportion to that ownership of the entity: “withholdee” means a person receiving or entitled to receive a payment from which tax is required to be withheld under Division [] of Part VIII; “withholding agent” means a person required to withhold tax from a payment wnoder Division 1] of Part VII and "year of assessment” has the meaning assigned in section 18 11! body: Personal reliefs (section 31) 1. The personal reliefs referred to in section 51 are as follows: fa) (b) fc} (a) fe} @ in the case of an individual who has a dependant spouse or at least two dependant children, that individual is enniled to a personal relief of two hundred currency points; in the case of an individual who has a disability, that individual is entitled to a personal relief of twenty-five percent of the assessable income of that individual from a business or employment; in the case of an individual who is sixty years of age and above, that individual is entitled to a personal relief of two hundred currency points; in the case of an individual who is sponsoring the educa- tion of the child or ward of that individual in a recognised Tegistered educational institution in the country, that individual is entitled to a personal relief of two hundred currency points per child or ward up to a maximum of three children or wards; in the case of an individual who has a dependant relative, other than a child or spouse, who is sixty years of ape or more, that individual is entitled to a personal relief of one hundred currency pomts but thatindividual may only claim relief in respect of two dependant relatives; and m the case of an individual who has undergone trammme to update the professional, technical or vocational skills or knowledge of the individual, that individual is entitled to a personal relief which is equivalent to the cost of the training af not more than four hundred currency points. 2. Where two or more persons qualify in respect of the same child, ward or relative under paragraph 1 fd or fe), the Commiussioner-General shall grant only one relief. 3. For the purpose of this Schedule, “dependant child, spouse or relative” in respect of an individual, means a child, spouse or relative of the individual for whom that individual provides the necessities of life. 133

citation_label: Income Tax (Amendment) Act, 2019 (Act 1007) — Fifth Schedule personal-relief amounts substituted
heading: Income Tax (Amendment) Act, 2019 (Act 1007) — Fifth Schedule personal-relief amounts substituted

metadata.amends: Income Tax Act, 2015 (Act 896), Fifth Schedule paragraph 1 (personal-relief amounts), First Schedule paragraph 1 rate table (superseded by Act 1071 and Act 1111), Sixth Schedule (automotive concession inserted)
metadata.completeness_note: Two-page scan carrying the complete operative text (long title, sections 1-3, assent and gazette date lines). The arrangement-of-sections cover page of the print is not included in the scan.
metadata.hosting_note: Parliament of Ghana DSpace repository and GRA do not host Act 1007 (checked 2026-07-06); GhaLII hosts the same print at https://ghalii.org/akn/gh/act/2019/1007/eng@2019-12-30/source.pdf behind bot protection that blocks non-interactive capture. This capture uses a faithful scan of the Assembly Press print from the Sage HR Africa payroll-compliance document library. Authenticity features on the face of the print - the act-number recital (THE ONE THOUSAND AND SEVENTH ACT), date of assent 28 December 2019 and Gazette notification 30 December 2019 - match the GhaLII catalogue record and the judy.legal AKN record for Act 1007.
metadata.not_superseded_note: No later Income Tax (Amendment) Act amends the Fifth Schedule. Checked on 2026-07-06 against Act 1017 (section 94 only), Act 1066 (Sixth Schedule), Act 1071 (First/Second/Sixth Schedules), Act 1094 (sections plus First/Fourth Schedules), Act 1111 (First Schedule paragraph 1) and Act 1134 (sections 6, 85, 115, 119 and First Schedule paragraph 8). The Act 1007 relief amounts are the amounts in force for the 2026 year of assessment.
metadata.publisher: Ghana Publishing Company Limited (Assembly Press), Accra
metadata.source_authority: Parliament of the Republic of Ghana (Assembly Press print; third-party hosted scan)
metadata.source_note: Section 2 substitutes the Fifth Schedule paragraph 1 amounts: paragraph 1(a) marriage/responsibility relief two hundred to one thousand two hundred currency points; 1(c) old-age relief two hundred to one thousand five hundred currency points; 1(d) child-education relief two hundred to six hundred currency points per child or ward (maximum three unchanged); 1(e) aged-dependant-relative relief one hundred to one thousand currency points (maximum two dependants unchanged); 1(f) professional/technical/vocational training relief cap four hundred to two thousand currency points. Paragraph 1(b) disability relief (25 percent of assessable income) is not amended. Cross-checked against the GRA Personal Tax Relief surface (GH¢1,200 / GH¢1,500 / GH¢600 / GH¢1,000 / GH¢2,000 / 25 percent) on 2026-07-06.
metadata.title: Income Tax (Amendment) Act, 2019 (Act 1007) — Fifth Schedule personal-relief amounts substituted

body:
Act 1007

REPUBLIC OF GHANA

THE ONE THOUSAND AND SEVENTH

ACT

OF THE PARLIAMENT OF THE REPUBLIC OF GHANA ENTITLED

INCOME TAX (AMENDMENT) ACT, 2019

AN ACT to amend the Income Tax Act, 2015 (Act 896) to revise the rates of tax for the chargeable income for a resident individual for a year of assessment, provide for a tax holiday for automobile manufacturers and assemblers and for related matters.

DATE OF ASSENT: 28" December, 2019. PASSED by Parliament and assented to by the President:

First Schedule to Act 896 amended

1. The Income Tax Act, 2015 (Act 896) referred to in this Act as the “principal enactment” is amended in the First Schedule by the substitution for the table in subparagraph (1) of paragraph 1, of

“

NO. CHARGEABLE INCOME RATE OF TAX

L, First GH¢3,828 Nil

2: Next GH¢1,200 5 percent

3. Next GH¢1,440 10 percent

4, Next GH¢36,000 17.5 percent

5. Next GH¢197,532 25 percent

6. Exceeding GH¢240,000 30 percent ,

2

Incomer Tax (Amendment) Act, 2019 Act 1007

Fifth Schedule to Act 896 amended

2. The principal enactment is amended in the Fifth Schedule by the substitution for

fa)

(b) () @ (e)

“two hundred currency points” in subparagraph (a) of paragraph | of “one thousand two hundred currency points”;

“two hundred currency points” in subparagraph (c) of paragraph | of “one thousand five hundred currency points”; “two hundred currency points” in subparagraph (d) of paragraph | of “six hundred currency points”;

“one hundred currency points” in subparagraph (e) of paragraph 1 of “one thousand currency points”; and “four hundred currency points”; in subparagraph () of paragraph | of “two thousand currency points.”.

Sixth Schedule to Act 896 amended 3. The principal enactment is amended in the Sixth Schedule by the insertion after paragraph 11 of

“Registered manufacturers and assemblers of automobiles under the Ghana Automotive Manufacturing Development Programme 12. (1) The income ofa manufacturer or assembler in respect of the manufacture or assembling of (a) semi-knocked down vehicles is exempt from tax for a period of three years from the date of commencement of the manufacturing or assembling business; and (®) complete-knocked down vehicles is exempt from tax for a period of ten years from the date of commencement of the manufacturing or (2) The concessions provided for in subparagraph (1) are cumulative.

(3) A manufacturer or assembler who starts with semi- knocked down vehicles and converts to complete-knocked down vehicles is not entitled to a concessionary period greater than an aggregate of ten years.”.

Date of Gazette notification: 30° December, 2019. 3

citation_label: Income Tax (Amendment) Act, 2025 (Act 1134) — lottery and unprocessed-gold withholding removed
heading: Income Tax (Amendment) Act, 2025 (Act 1134) — lottery and unprocessed-gold withholding removed

metadata.act_number_note: The Assembly Press print reads Act 1134 (THE ONE THOUSAND ONE HUNDRED AND THIRTY-FOURTH ACT). Several published tax alerts (for example EY, March 2025) refer to the same amendment as Act 1129; the print's own act number is recorded here as authoritative. The corpus provision gh/statute/act-1111/... not_superseded_note and the rulespec-gh Act 1111 fixture comment cite the 2025 amendment as Act 1129 following those alerts.
metadata.amends: Income Tax Act, 2015 (Act 896), sections 6, 85, 115, 119 and First Schedule paragraph 8 (withholding items)
metadata.publisher: Ghana Publishing Company Limited (Assembly Press), Accra
metadata.scope_note: Removes withholding on lottery winnings and on purchases of unprocessed gold. The First Schedule change deletes paragraph 8 subparagraph 1(b)(x); the paragraph 1(1) individual rate bands (substituted by Act 1111) and the Fifth Schedule personal reliefs (amounts substituted by Act 1007) are untouched, so both remain in force for the 2026 year of assessment.
metadata.source_authority: Parliament of the Republic of Ghana
metadata.source_note: Authentic Assembly Press printed Act 1134, downloaded from the Parliament of Ghana DSpace repository ORIGINAL bitstream on 2026-07-06. Text-embedded PDF.
metadata.title: Income Tax (Amendment) Act, 2025 (Act 1134) — lottery and unprocessed-gold withholding removed

body:
INCOME TAX (AKC^OlEI^TrACT/ZO^1 - Act 1134 ARRANGEMENT OF SECTIONS Section 1r Section 6 of Act 896 amended 2. Section 85 of Act 896 amended 3. Section 115 of Act 896 amended 4. Section 119 of Act 896 amended 5. First Schedule to Act 896 amended

Act 1134 REPUBLIC OF GHANA The One Thousand one hundred and thirty-fourth ACT OF THE PARLIAMENT OF THE REPUBLIC OF GHANA ENTITLED INCOME TAX (AMENDMENT) ACT, 2025 AN ACT to amend the Income Tax Act, 2015 (Act 896) to remove the withholding on winnings from lottery and on purchase of unprocessed gold and to provide for related matters. DATE OF AS SENT: 2nd April, 2025 PASSED by Parliament and assented to by the President Section 6 of Act 896 amended 1. The Income Tax Act, 2015 (Act 896), referred to in this Act as the “principal enactment”, is amended in section 6 by the deletion of subparagraph (vii) of paragraph (a) of subsection (2). Section 85 of Act 896 amended 2. The principal enactment is amended in subsection (5) of section 85 by the substitution for the definition of “unprocessed precious mineral”, of ““unprocessed precious mineral” means unprocessed rough diamonds within the meaning given in the 2

Income Tax (Amendment) Act, 2025 Act 1134 Minerals and Mining Act, 2006 (Act 703) and other minerals as may be prescribed by Regulations.”. Section 115 of Act 896 amended 3. The principal enactment is amended in section 115 by the substitution for subsection (1), of “(1) Subject to subsection (2), a resident person shall withhold tax at the rate specified in paragraph 8 of the First Schedule where that person pays (a) any dividend, interest, natural resource payment, rent or royalty to another person, or (b) consideration to another person in respect of the realisation of an asset or a liability and the payment has a source in the country.”. Section 119 of Act 896 amended 4. The principal enactment is amended in section 119 by the deletion of paragraph (f) of subsection (1). First Schedule to Act 896 amended 5. The principal enactment is amended in paragraph 8 by the deletion of subparagraph l(b)(x). Date of Gazette notification: 2nd April, 2025. 3

metadata.act_number_note: The Assembly Press print reads Act 1134 (THE ONE THOUSAND ONE HUNDRED AND THIRTY-FOURTH ACT). Several published tax alerts (for example EY, March 2025) refer to the same amendment as Act 1129; the print's own act number is recorded here as authoritative. The corpus provision gh/statute/act-1111/... not_superseded_note and the rulespec-gh Act 1111 fixture comment cite the 2025 amendment as Act 1129 following those alerts.
metadata.amends: Income Tax Act, 2015 (Act 896), sections 6, 85, 115, 119 and First Schedule paragraph 8 (withholding items)
metadata.publisher: Ghana Publishing Company Limited (Assembly Press), Accra
metadata.scope_note: Removes withholding on lottery winnings and on purchases of unprocessed gold. The First Schedule change deletes paragraph 8 subparagraph 1(b)(x); the paragraph 1(1) individual rate bands (substituted by Act 1111) and the Fifth Schedule personal reliefs (amounts substituted by Act 1007) are untouched, so both remain in force for the 2026 year of assessment.

metadata.source_note: Income Tax (Amendment) Act, 2016 (Act 907) from the Parliament of Ghana DSpace repository ORIGINAL bitstream, downloaded 2026-07-07. Text-embedded PDF (no OCR). Section 1 inserts section 7(1)(p) and (q) of Act 896 (interest paid to an individual by a resident financial institution or on Government of Ghana bonds, and unit-trust/ mutual-fund interest and dividends, exempt). Section 2 substitutes First Schedule paragraphs 8(1)(b)(viii) (lottery nil-band, later deleted by Act 956), 8(1)(c)(vii) (7.5 percent service fees) and 8(3) (three percent, later substituted by Act 1071 and removed by Act 1134). The window captures sections 1-2 and stops before section 3 (Sixth Schedule).
metadata.title: Income Tax (Amendment) Act, 2016 (Act 907) — exempt interest and First Schedule substitutions

body: The Income Tax Act, 2015 (Act 896) referred to in this Act as the “principal enactment” is amended in subsection (1) of section 7 (a) by the deletion of “and” after paragraph (n); (b) by the deletion of “.” and the insertion of “; and” after paragraph (o); and (c) by the insertion of new paragraphs (p) and (q) as follows: “(p) interest paid to an individual (i) by a resident financial institution; or (ii) on bonds issued by the Government of Ghana; and “(q) the interest or dividend paid or credited to a holder or member on an investment in an approved unit trust scheme or mutual fund is exempt”.

body: Modified Taxation (Section 15) Principles of modified taxation 1, This Schedule modifies the taxation of eligible resident individuals by fa) imposing a presumptive tax on individuals thar only have income from the businesses specified in paragraphs 3 and 4; and (i) applying a modified cash basis in calculating income from the businesses specified in paragraph 5. Presumptive taxation 2. (1) Presumptive taxation applies where (a) the chargeable income of a resident mdividual for a year of assessment consists exclusively of income from a business; (#) the income is exclusively from sources within Ghana; and fe) the mdividual (i) is not registered for value added tax purposes and has an annual turnover of not more than twenty thousand Cedis from the business, computed as an average Of the turnever for three consecutive years ending in the year of assessment; or (1) has an annual turnover of more than twenty thousand Cedis from the business and is not required to register for value added tax purposes. (2) For purposes of subparagraph (1)/c), where the average turnover of an individual is not available for the period specified, the Com- mussioner-General may determine how the turnover is to be calculated. Exclusions from presumptive tax 3. (1) The following individuals are excluded from presumptive taxation under paragraphs 4 and 5, even if they meet the requirements of paragraph 2: fa) an individual who has a professional qualification; (6) an individual who is engaged in a business prescribed by regulations that has a high profit to tumoever ratio; fe} an individual who has more than one business f@) an individual who has a business with more than one business outlet fe) an individual in a parmership; and 121 Act 896 INCOME TAX ACT, 20/5 (Pan individual who elects to disapply paragraphs <4 and 5 for a year of assessment. (2) Where an individual elects to disapply paragraphs 4 and 5, paragraphs 4 and 5 shall not apply to that individual for that year of assessment and the following five years of assessment, but that individual may qualify for modified cash basis taxation under paragraph 6. Presumptive tax based on instalments 4. Where presumptive taxation applies to an individual as referred to in paragraph 2(1)feX1), the tax payable by that individual for a year of assessment tinder section 1{Viye) is the total of instalments payable by that individual for a year of assessment under section 121. Presumptive tax based on turnover 5. Where presumptive taxation applies to an individual as referred to im paragraph 2(1)feXi), the tax payable by that individual for a year of assessment under section [(1)f@) is three percent of the turnover of the business, where the turnover is more than twenty thousand cedis but does not exceed one hundred and twenty thousand cedis. Modified cash basis 6. (1) The modified cash basis under subparagraph (2) applies where fa) the assessable income of a resident individual for a year of assessment from all businesses conducted by that individual consists exclusively of income from sources in the country; and (i) the turnover of that individual does not exceed one hundred and twenty thousand cedis, calculated using the modified cash basis. (2) Where the modified cash basis applies as referred to in subparagraph (1) the income of an individual from a business for a year of assessment shall be calculated fa) according to the standard rules for calculating come from a business; anc fi) using the modified cash basis of accounting. (3) For the purpose of subparagraph (2)(6), sections 17, 21 (4), 25, 26 and 31 donot apply to the calculation of the income of an individual from a business for a year of assessment. (4) In this Schedule, “turnover of a business for a year" means the amount derived from the business during the year that ts required to be included in calculating income from the business under section 3(2\¥a), (itu) and (wu) only. 122

Paragraphs 2(1)(c)(i), 5 and 6(1)(b) were substituted by Act 902 and again by Act 1071; cite the current band amounts from the Act 1071 provision, not this base text. The three percent rate is common to every version.

citation_label: COVID-19 Health Recovery Levy Act, 2021 (Act 1068) — imposition and one per cent rate
heading: COVID-19 Health Recovery Levy Act, 2021 (Act 1068) — imposition and one per cent rate

metadata.original_publisher: Ghana Publishing Company Limited (Assembly Press), Accra
metadata.source_authority: Ghana Revenue Authority (hosting the enacted Act)
metadata.source_note: COVID-19 Health Recovery Levy Act, 2021 (Act 1068) PDF hosted by the Ghana Revenue Authority, downloaded 2026-07-07. Section 1 imposes the Levy on the supply of goods and services and on imports; section 1(4) sets the rate at one per cent of the value of the taxable supply. GHAMOD applies this one per cent on the turnover of presumptive-tax payers (ttn02_s); the statutory base is the taxable supply, recorded for the parity suite's scope note.
metadata.title: COVID-19 Health Recovery Levy Act, 2021 (Act 1068) — imposition and one per cent rate

body: 1. (1) There is imposed by this Act a COVID-19 Health Recovery Levy on the (a) supply of goods or services made in the country other than exempt goods or services; and (b) import of goods or import of services other than exempt imports. (2) The time of supply is the same as provided in the Value Added Tax Act, 2013 (Act 870). Covid-19 Health Recovery Levy Act, 2021 Act 1068 (3) A person who charges the Value Added Tax flat rate shall also charge the Levy on the supply of goods or services. (4) Except as otherwise provided in this Act, the rate of the Levy is One per cent and is calculated on the value of the taxable supply of the goods or services or on the value of the imports. (5) The Levy is subject to review by the Minister.
citation_label: COVID-19 Health Recovery Levy Act, 2021 (Act 1068) — imposition and one per cent rate 1
heading: section-1-imposition-of-levy
